ENGNew: We Can Wear Whatever We Want?
changeReason: Improved translation from Japanese. Old: "We Don't Have a Uniform?"

TextENGNew: It was still only June... and yet it was already sweltering.
Cicadas cried mii-mii outside, and at night the mosquitoes came out.@k ...This was summer in every sense but the calendar.
changeReason: Old incorrectly identifies the insects as "higurashi" but the Japanese says セミ (generic cicadas) making ミンミン sounds, characteristic of minmin-zemi, not higurashi.

TextENGNew: ...At least the mornings were still cool. That was the one saving grace.
``It is dreadfully hot, I must say!``
changeReason: Old paraphrases as "Summer, I do declare!" but the Japanese specifically says 暑い (hot); new correctly translates the complaint about heat.

TextENGNew: Satoko was fanning herself with her skirt in a listless sort of way.
...That's not very ladylike, I have to say.
...Brat or not, she's still a girl.
``Keiichi-san looks so cool in just a shirt... I envy you.``
``From where I stand, your skirt looks like the cooler option. You couldn't possibly understand how stifling trousers are at this time of year!``
changeReason: "Jeans" changed to "trousers" -- Japanese says ズボン (generic trousers/pants), not jeans specifically.

TextENGNew: This girl's imagination has gone somewhere strange again...
changeReason: "Indecent" changed to "strange" -- 妙な means strange/peculiar, not necessarily indecent; the old translation adds a connotation not in the original.

TextENGNew: ``Rena's summer clothes have such a nice, cool color. Just looking at them makes me feel a bit cooler.``
``Ahahaha. Thank you!@k They really are quite cool.``
``I do wish I had cool summer clothes like Rena-san's.``
``But Satoko-chan's summer dress is incredibly cute! Rena would love to try wearing it☆.``
``This is actually quite muggy, you know? I'm sure yours is the cooler option, Rena-san.``
``But cute clothes make everything more fun, I'm sure of it... Hau!``
changeReason: "Hao" corrected to "Hau" (Japanese はぅ); also "kyute" normalized to "cute" losing the deliberate misspelling that conveyed Rena's cutesy pronunciation of かぁいい.

TextENGNew: ...I had the sense that Rena and Satoko operated on fundamentally different values.
``Come to think of it... this school doesn't have a designated uniform, does it.``
``Right. It doesn't.@k As long as what you wear is appropriate, regular clothes are perfectly fine.``
Sure enough, many students wore their own clothes. Those in uniforms all wore the same design—plain and unremarkable.
``...What's the uniform the others are wearing, though? They're all identical.``
``That's the uniform from the school in town. There's no rule about it, but everyone wears it just the same.``
``In contrast, all our friends are wearing different uniforms. Did you actually go out of your way to get them from somewhere?``
``Indeed. Mion-san procures them for us.``
``Mii-chan has a relative who runs a secondhand clothing shop. Apparently they stock school uniforms from all over the country at very low prices.``
``And so you put in a request to those relatives for a variety of distinctive uniforms.``
...That Mion, she absolutely loves dressing her friends up like paper dolls.
changeReason: "Dress-up dolls" changed to "paper dolls" -- 着せ替え人形 specifically means dress-up dolls (fashion dolls whose clothes can be changed), not paper dolls; the new translation is less accurate.

TextENGNew: ...What a peculiar secondhand shop, though.
I could understand secondhand clothing in general—but school uniforms from all over the country?
...A very strange shop indeed.
Uniforms from distant schools nobody's ever heard of—what use could those possibly be??
``...Yeah, Rena thinks so too. They also carry secondhand gym clothes and school swimsuits.@k ...Used ones are a bit off-putting, honestly.``
``Doesn't sound like much of a money-maker... Mion probably pitches in to help them scrape together a bit of profit.``
``...But Mion-san always says with complete confidence—'One day it'll be a massive hit and a wonderful business!!'``
...A secondhand shop specializing in school uniforms becoming a massive hit?
...I don't see it.
